source: Invest Like the Best — Investing through a bear market (EP.167)
summary: Valuation regime shift from EV/Sales to GAAP P/E is the single biggest risk for growth investors
summary_extended: Baker identifies the single largest risk he faces as a growth investor as a valuation regime shift — specifically, the transition from companies being valued on EV/Sales multiples to being valued on GAAP P/E multiples. When this shift occurs, high-growth companies with little or no GAAP earnings can experience severe multiple compression regardless of their underlying business quality, as the market reprices them on a profitability basis they cannot yet support. Baker states he thinks about this risk 'all the time' and actively tries to mitigate it through portfolio construction, favoring companies that are closer to or already generating GAAP earnings. He notes the risk is particularly difficult to quantify or hedge precisely, making it a qualitative but central concern in how he sizes and selects positions across his growth-oriented portfolio.
detail: Baker identifies a regime shift where companies go from being valued on EV/Sales to GAAP P/E as the single largest risk he thinks about as a growth investor. He actively tries to mitigate this risk in portfolio construction. Notes it is difficult to capture quantitatively.
confidence_evidence: Baker explicitly calls it 'the biggest single risk to me' and says he thinks about it 'all the time.' Framed as a core portfolio construction concern.
quote_fragment: biggest single risk to me is a regime shift in valuations
